task: Review one no-write LLM Editor planning brief for site fit, risk, and readiness.
prompt: Act as the LLM Reviewer gate for lastzguides.com. Review the planning brief only. Do not write or rewrite public page copy. Do not create patch specs. Check duplicate intent, cluster role separation, canonical claim protection, template safety, owner questions, and deterministic QA readiness. Treat analytics as signals, not proof. Return JSON only.

selected_opportunity.rationale: Owner approved this topic for one no-write LLM worker-chain replay only. This does not approve public content edits, patch specs, PRs, or deployment.
selected_opportunity.player_value: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for `Last Z HQ Upgrade Guide — Requirements, Fast Path, and HQ 30/35 Strategy` searchers.
selected_opportunity.duplication_risk: Review against the current cluster role before any content proposal.
selected_opportunity.next_step: Run the no-write Editor and Reviewer stages from this approved decision snapshot.

current_page_snapshot.title: Last Z HQ Upgrade Guide (2026) — Requirements, Fast Path to HQ 30/35
current_page_snapshot.h1: Last Z HQ Upgrade Guide — Requirements, Fast Path, and HQ 30/35 Strategy
current_page_snapshot.meta_description: Best HQ strategy for Last Z: rush early levels, upgrade required buildings only, push efficiently to HQ30, and prepare steel for HQ31-35.
current_page_snapshot.quick_answer: Best Last Z HQ upgrade path for most players: rush early levels, upgrade only required buildings, push efficiently to HQ30 first, and treat HQ31-35 as a separate steel-based progression phase with much heavier timers.
h2_headings: Why HQ Level Matters: Hero Level Cap and Progression
HQ Upgrade Requirements: What Buildings Actually Matter

llm_editor_brief.brief_summary: Keep hq.html as the cornerstone progression guide, but tighten the opening to answer the HQ upgrade query immediately, then structure the page around fast-path leveling, required-building decisions, and the HQ30→35 steel phase without shifting cluster role.
llm_editor_brief.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for Last Z HQ Upgrade Guide — Requirements, Fast Path, and HQ 30/35 Strategy searchers.
llm_editor_brief.first_screen_plan: Preserve answer-first layout. The first screen should immediately confirm the page solves HQ upgrade requirements and fast-path strategy, then signal the split between early rush progression and the heavier HQ31–35 steel phase. Keep the existing template and avoid introducing a different content mission or persona. The opening should help users decide whether to rush, what to upgrade now, and when the guide changes after HQ30.

section_plan.action: Keep it as the immediate answer block and make sure it names the best overall HQ path, the required-building rule, and the HQ30/35 split.
section_plan.reason: This is the highest-intent section and should resolve the query before users scroll.

section_plan.action: Frame the recommended route as a practical default for most players, with clear conditions for when to deviate.
section_plan.reason: Searchers need a concise decision, not just raw requirements.

section_plan.action: Organize the choice points by early-game speed, required upgrade dependencies, and whether the player is pre- or post-HQ30.
section_plan.reason: The query implies planning tradeoffs, so this section should reduce ambiguity.

section_plan.action: Keep the internal route block and ensure it points to adjacent progression topics instead of replacing the page’s role.
section_plan.reason: Supports navigation while protecting cluster separation.

section_plan.action: Use this area to connect HQ strategy with adjacent progression questions and common follow-ups.
section_plan.reason: Improves satisfaction and internal pathing without overexpanding the cornerstone guide.

internal_link_plan.reason: Entry point for broader site navigation and progression discovery.
Matches the upstream route and supports users who are still orienting early progression choices.
Adjacent planning topic for players deciding how aggressively to progress before HQ milestones.
Useful companion for upgrade sequencing and building priority decisions.
Related progression-adjacent content that can answer broader early-game route questions.
Specific tactical follow-up that benefits players after understanding HQ progression timing.
Downstream optimization topic for players who have moved beyond core HQ planning.
Supporting guide for a concrete building-cost or upgrade concern likely encountered during HQ planning.

do_not_change: Do not alter the page into a start-page replacement or broader onboarding guide.
Do not change the existing page template, navigation pattern, or schema family.
Do not remove the cornerstone-guide role or blur the Progression cluster boundary.
Do not create a new page or split the topic into multiple pages.
Do not introduce unverifiable claims about HQ requirements, costs, or timings without canonical source confirmation.
Do not use analytics metrics as proof that a rewrite is required.
owner_questions: Should the opening answer explicitly mention HQ30 as the main breakpoint, or keep that detail deeper in the page?
Do you want the decision framework to lean more toward speed-rush guidance or resource-efficiency guidance?
Are there any canonical HQ requirement details that must remain verbatim in the requirements section?
acceptance_checks: manual review: first-screen answer and internal links on hq.html
llm_editor_brief.next_step: Run the no-write Editor and Reviewer stages against hq.html, verifying that the first screen answers the query immediately and that internal links preserve the Progression cluster routing.

guardrails: No content files may be edited by Reviewer.
No final public page copy may be generated.
No patch specs, backlog entries, manifests, PRs, or production changes may be created.
High-risk cornerstone/home pages cannot be advanced without explicit owner review.
Owner approval is required before any user-visible content change.
